events.text: Patient is a 48-year-old female of White/Caucasian race with an ECOG performance status of 0
At age 48, patient presented with painless right axillary swelling for two months, no B symptoms, found to have isolated right axillary lymphadenopathy on examination
At age 48, excisional biopsy of the right axillary lymph node revealed diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, ICD-O site C77.9, histology code 9680/3

At age 48, pathology report demonstrated centroblastic morphology with starry-sky pattern, Ki-67 of 92 percent, immunohistochemistry positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, BCL2 negative, MUM1 negative, MYC protein positive in 65 percent of cells, consistent with GCB subtype by Hans algorithm
At age 48, flow cytometry showed monoclonal B-cell population strongly positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, negative for CD5, CD23, MUM1

At age 48, FISH analysis showed MYC rearrangement positive with IGH-MYC fusion, BCL2 rearrangement negative, BCL6 rearrangement positive; classified as double-hit lymphoma (MYC and BCL6), reclassified as high-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL6 rearrangements
At age 48, laboratory workup showed LDH elevated at 445 U/L, CBC with WBC 6.9, hemoglobin 13.4 g/dL, platelets 256, CMP unremarkable, uric acid 7.2 mg/dL
At age 48, staging PET/CT showed FDG-avid right axillary lymphadenopathy with largest node 7.4 cm (bulky), SUVmax 25.1, and FDG-avid right supraclavicular node 2.3 cm, no disease elsewhere
At age 48, bone marrow biopsy was negative for lymphoma involvement with normal trilineage hematopoiesis

At age 48, patient was staged as Ann Arbor Stage II bulky (right axillary and ipsilateral supraclavicular), no B symptoms, IPI score 2 (low-intermediate risk), however double-hit biology confers higher risk

At age 48, given double-hit biology, consideration given to dose-adjusted R-EPOCH versus R-CHOP; after discussion, patient initiated cycle 1 of R-CHOP at standard doses with plan for close interim assessment
At age 48, after cycle 1 of R-CHOP, patient experienced grade 1 nausea and grade 1 fatigue only
At age 48, patient received cycle 2 of R-CHOP with pegfilgrastim, no significant toxicity
At age 48, patient received cycle 3 of R-CHOP on schedule
At age 48, interim PET/CT after 3 cycles showed dramatic response with right axillary mass reduced to 1.8 cm, SUVmax 3.2, Deauville score 3, supraclavicular node resolved
At age 48, encouraged by interim response, patient completed cycles 4, 5, and 6 of R-CHOP without dose modifications or significant toxicity
At age 48, end-of-treatment PET/CT demonstrated complete metabolic response, Deauville score 1, no residual FDG-avid disease anywhere
At age 48, patient achieved complete remission after 6 cycles of R-CHOP and was transitioned to surveillance with close monitoring given double-hit biology
At age 49, surveillance PET/CT at 6 months showed no evidence of recurrence
At age 49, surveillance CT at 12 months remained without evidence of disease
At age 49, patient reports resolution of all treatment-related side effects, LDH normalized at 172 U/L, remains in complete remission
At age 50, surveillance CT at 24 months showed no evidence of recurrence, patient transitioned to annual surveillance

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 48, pathology report demonstrated centroblastic morphology with starry-sky pattern, Ki-67 of 92 percent, immunohistochemistry positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, BCL2 negative, MUM1 negative, MYC protein positive in 65 percent of cells, consistent with GCB subtype by Hans algorithm

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

At age 48, flow cytometry showed monoclonal B-cell population strongly positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, negative for CD5, CD23, MUM1

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), germinal center and activated B-cell subtypes. Ann Arbor stage II-IV. Treated with R-CHOP. Include IPI scoring, PET-CT response assessment, some with relapse requiring R-ICE salvage and autologous stem cell transplant.
